description: The figure presents inverse pole figures (IPFs) showing the distribution of grain orientations along the tensile axis (TA) direction in an austenitic Fe30Mn6.5Al0.3C low-density steel, comparing uncharged (a) and hydrogen-charged (101 mass ppm H, b) samples deformed to the end of uniform elongation (ε = 0.4). The stereographic triangles span crystallographic directions from <001>//TA (bottom left) to <111>//TA (top) to <101>//TA (bottom right), with the [112] direction marked along the left edge. Individual grain orientations are plotted as colored symbols representing four dislocation structure types classified via ECCI analysis: Type 1a (black circles, cell blocks with primary and secondary GNB systems formed under single/two-fold slip along [112]-[101]), Type 1b (gray circles, cell blocks delimited by two GNB sets), Type 2 (red circles, dislocation cells near <001>//TA), and Type 3 (blue circles, mixed structures with microbands near <111>//TA). The uncharged sample shows Type 1a dominating the [112]-[101] region, while the hydrogen-charged sample exhibits an expanded Type 1b field and modified Type 1a distribution, indicating hydrogen-enhanced microbanding and altered GNB development. Specific Miller indices (e.g., [6 3 14], [4 4 7], [5 3 7]) label evaluated grain orientations, demonstrating the crystallographic dependence of deformation structures and hydrogen's role in promoting localized strain accommodation.
